successCriteria.description: `src/shared/types/leave.types.ts` exists and exports all 7 enums with correct members: LeaveType (ANNUAL, SICK, MATERNITY, PATERNITY, UNPAID, OTHER), LeaveStatus (PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, CANCELLED), NotificationType (LEAVE_REQUESTED, LEAVE_APPROVED, LEAVE_REJECTED, LEAVE_CANCELLED), AuditAction (CREATED, UPDATED, DELETED, APPROVED, REJECTED, CANCELLED), EntityType (LEAVE_REQUEST, LEAVE_BALANCE, LEAVE_POLICY, EMPLOYEE), EmployeeRole (EMPLOYEE, MANAGER, HR_ADMIN), EmploymentStatus (ACTIVE, INACTIVE, TERMINATED, ON_LEAVE)

`src/shared/error.types.ts` exists and exports 4 error classes: NotFoundError (extends Error, constructor takes entityName: string, id: string), ValidationError (extends Error, constructor takes message: string, optional fieldErrors: Array<{ field: string; message: string }>), ForbiddenError (extends Error, constructor takes message: string), ConflictError (extends Error, constructor takes message: string)

`src/shared/base.repository.ts` exists and exports an abstract class `BaseRepository<T>` that accepts a Knex instance in its constructor and declares abstract method signatures: `findById(id: string): Promise<T | null>`, `findAll(filter?: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<T[]>`, `create(entity: Partial<T>): Promise<T>`, `update(id: string, entity: Partial<T>): Promise<T>`, `delete(id: string): Promise<void>`

`tests/unit/shared/error.types.test.ts` exists with Vitest unit tests covering: NotFoundError (correct name, message, entityName/id properties), ValidationError (correct name, message, fieldErrors when provided and when omitted), ForbiddenError (correct name, message), ConflictError (correct name, message). All tests pass.

constraints: All TypeScript enums must use string enum values (not numeric), consistent with TypeScript strict mode and the project's no-implicit-any constraint
BaseRepository<T> must accept a Knex instance via constructor injection — the Knex type must be imported from the knex package (already in package.json as dependency ^3.1.0)
Error classes must properly extend Error with `Object.setPrototypeOf(this, ClassName.prototype)` in their constructors to preserve the prototype chain in TypeScript when targeting ES5/ES2015
Tests must use Vitest (per AGENTS.md and ARCHITECTURE.md), not Jest — despite HARNESS.json declaring Jest, the more specific directives in AGENTS.md and ARCHITECTURE.md take precedence
All files must use strict TypeScript (no implicit any, strict null checks) per AGENTS.md
No database calls in shared types or error classes — these are pure TypeScript definitions with zero runtime dependencies beyond the standard library and knex types
outOfScope: Phase 3 — LeavePolicy model, repository, service, and index (src/modules/policy/)
Phase 6 — AuditLog and Notification models and repositories
Phase 10 — Leave routes with RBAC middleware
Knex configuration/migration setup — the BaseRepository accepts a Knex instance via constructor injection but does not create or configure one
Database schema, migrations, or seed data for any leave management tables
Any runtime behavior beyond type/enum definitions and error class instantiation — no services, controllers, routes, or middleware in this phase

ambiguities.description: The intent says "Use the Knex type from the existing project setup" but no Knex configuration or usage exists in `src/` yet (only the `knex` npm dependency in package.json). The Knex type must be imported from the `knex` package itself — likely `import type { Knex } from 'knex'` — but the exact import path is not specified.

The exact method signatures for `BaseRepository<T>` are not fully specified — only method names are given (findById, findAll, create, update, delete). Parameter types and return types must be inferred. The ARCHITECTURE.md shows repository interfaces with methods like `findById(id: string): Promise<T | null>` and `findAll(filter?: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<T[]>` which provide a reasonable template.
options: Use signatures matching ARCHITECTURE.md repository patterns
Use generic signatures with `Partial<T>` for create/update

ambiguities.description: The `ValidationError` constructor takes an "optional fieldErrors array" but the shape of each element in that array is not specified. A common pattern is `Array<{ field: string; message: string }>` but this is not explicit in the intent.
